result.stdout:
{"assemblyFileName":"GitVersionInfo.Tool.dll","version":"1.0.0.0","fileVersion":"1.0.0.0","informationalVersion":"git-6f4c726-master--2020-07-03"}

GitVersionInfo options:
  Add:  Adds git metadata information to the DotNet project that will be embedded into the assembly after build.
  Get:  Returns the git metadata in json format. This allows creating CI/CD scripts that may use that information.

Parameters:
  --Directory: Sets the directory of the repository to add the git version, by default it is the working directory.
  --AssemblyInformationalFormat: Customize the free text that will be written to the assembly's informational version (product version).
                                 Use the '{GitVersionInfo}' variable to place the info generated by this tool. 
                                 Example: 'GeneralVersion 123 - {GitVersionInfo} demo.'
                                 The default is: 'git-{ShortSha}-{BranchName}--{CommitDate} {PendingChanges}'.

Format template variables:
  {Sha}               : Commit SHA-1 id (40 characters).
  {ShortSha}          : Commit SHA-1 id (first 7 characters).
  {BranchName}        : Branch friendly name, i.e.: `master`.
  {CommitMessage}     : Commit message of the current commit.
  {CommitAuthorName}  : Display name of the author of the current commit.
  {CommitAuthorEmail} : Email of the author of the current commit.
  {CommitDate}        : Date in format 'yyy-MM-dd'.
  {PendingChanges}    : List of the pending changes used during the build.

Examples:
dotnet GitVersionInfo Get
dotnet GitVersionInfo Add
dotnet GitVersionInfo Add --Directory 'C:\SomeRepoFolder\'
dotnet GitVersionInfo Add --Directory 'C:\SomeRepoFolder\' --AssemblyInformationalFormat 'PresentationDemo-git-{ShortSha}-{BranchName}-{CommitDate}-{PendingChanges}'

{"assemblyFileName":"GitVersionInfo.Tool.dll","version":"1.0.0.0","fileVersion":"1.0.0.0","informationalVersion":"git-6f4c726-master--2020-07-03"}

GitVersionInfo options:
  Add:  Adds git metadata information to the DotNet project that will be embedded into the assembly after build.
  Get:  Returns the git metadata in json format. This allows creating CI/CD scripts that may use that information.

Parameters:
  --Directory: Sets the directory of the repository to add the git version, by default it is the working directory.
  --AssemblyInformationalFormat: Customize the free text that will be written to the assembly's informational version (product version).
                                 Use the '{GitVersionInfo}' variable to place the info generated by this tool. 
                                 Example: 'GeneralVersion 123 - {GitVersionInfo} demo.'
                                 The default is: 'git-{ShortSha}-{BranchName}--{CommitDate} {PendingChanges}'.

Format template variables:
  {Sha}               : Commit SHA-1 id (40 characters).
  {ShortSha}          : Commit SHA-1 id (first 7 characters).
  {BranchName}        : Branch friendly name, i.e.: `master`.
  {CommitMessage}     : Commit message of the current commit.
  {CommitAuthorName}  : Display name of the author of the current commit.
  {CommitAuthorEmail} : Email of the author of the current commit.
  {CommitDate}        : Date in format 'yyy-MM-dd'.
  {PendingChanges}    : List of the pending changes used during the build.

Examples:
dotnet GitVersionInfo Get
dotnet GitVersionInfo Add
dotnet GitVersionInfo Add --Directory 'C:\SomeRepoFolder\'
